figures.caption: Table 5: Model comparisons when pricing 0DTEs from May 6, 2022 to May 11, 2023. The RMSE is computed as in Eq. (16) with |T | = 1. Data source: CBOE.

Table 6: Model comparison in pricing ultra-short tenors between May 2022 and May 2023. The RMSE is computed as in (16) with |T | = 6. We employed the SINC method with Nf = 104 nodes for the fast Fourier approximation. Data source: CBOE

Figure 1: The figure reports volume (i.e., number of contracts traded) in SPX options as a fraction of total volume associated with various tenors: between 0 and 7 days (1w), between 8 and 30 days (1m), between 31 and 60 days (2m), between 61 and 120 days (6m) and between 121 and 365 days (12m). The data covers the period from 2014 to 2023. Data source: OptionMetrics.
Figure 7: We report the percentage of prices within the bid-ask spreads for each model across strikes and tenors. Data source: CBOE.
Figure 2: ATM implied-volatility term structure on a typical day: Thursday, August 18, 2022. Data source: CBOE.
Figure 5: Histograms of the daily RMSEs of estimated models in our samples on 0DTEs, from May 6, 2022 to May 11, 2023. Data source: CBOE.
Figure 8: Estimated implied-volatility smiles on August 18, 2022. The grey dashed lines denote the ATM level for the corresponding maturity. Data source: CBOE.

Figure 9: ATM implied-volatility term structure on 2 typical day, Thursday August 18, 2022 and Tuesday May 10, 2022. The colored dashed lines correspond to the fit of alternative models. Data source: CBOE.

Figure 12: Panel A: Histogram of the absolute error between the spot volatility estimates from 0DTEs and the spot volatility estimates from Edgeworth++ and 2F Heston Merton, respectively. Panel B: Histogram of the absolute error between the spot volatility estimates from 0DTEs and the spot volatility estimates from Edgeworth++ and 2F Heston Merton++, respectively. Data source: CBOE.
Figure 4: Number of options in each daily surface for each of the 6 maturities. The data covers the period May 6, 2022 to May 11, 2023. Data source: CBOE.

Figure 3: We report the histograms of two summary statistics for the ATM implied-volatility term structures associated with options with expiries between 0 and 7 calendar days (ultra-short-term options) and expiries between 8 and 30 calendar days. Data are from May 6, 2022 to May 11, 2023. Data source: CBOE. Panel A: Average absolute value of the first derivative of the ATM implied-volatility term structure, defined as 1 n−1 ∑n−1

Figure 11: Comparison of spot volatility estimates obtained at 10:30 on every day from May 6, 2022 to May 11, 2023. Panel A reports time-series estimates obtained with an EGARCH model, estimates from the Edgeworth model estimated on 0DTEs only, and estimates from the Edgeworth++ model estimated on surfaces with 6 maturities. Panel B reports time-series estimates obtained with an EGARCH model, estimates from the Rough Heston++ model and estimates from the 2F Heston Merton model. Panel C reports time-series estimates obtained with an EGARCH model, estimates from the Rough Heston Merton++ model and estimates from the 2F Heston Merton++ model. Data source: CBOE.
Figure 10: Estimated implied-volatility smiles on May 10, 2022. The grey dashed lines denote the ATM level for the corresponding maturity. Data source: CBOE.
Figure 6: Histograms of the daily RMSEs of estimated models on ultra-short-term implied-volatility surfaces, from May 6, 2022 to May 11, 2023. Data source: CBOE.
